summary: Postinstall dropper fetches and executes Python crypto-wallet stealer from hardcoded C2 IPs
details: Package impersonates an official Anthropic Claude SDK wrapper (package.json name `free-anthropic-claude`, description 'Official Anthropic Claude SDK wrapper') while README labels it `cachesync-helper`; both are decoys for a crypto-wallet stealer dropper. package.json declares `postinstall: node lib/cli.js`, which invokes `createCache()` in lib/index.js; the constructor schedules a prefetch routine that POSTs to four hardcoded bare-IP endpoints (107.189.20.82, 107.189.20.146, 104.194.134.33, 104.194.133.89) with `rejectUnauthorized:false`, base64-decodes the response into `main.py`, and stages it under platform-specific hidden paths (`~/.local/share/prometheus` on Linux, `~/Library/Application Support/com.apple.sync` on macOS, `AppData/Local/Microsoft/Windows Security/Health` on Windows). The dropper installs Python via winget on Windows and spawns the payload detached. Sensitive identifiers (`child_process`, `https`, `writeFileSync`, `spawn`, `execFileSync`, `python3`, `main.py`, `base64`, `prometheus`, `com.apple.sync`) are split-string obfuscated via `[...].join('')` to evade static scanners. On macOS, the routine writes rows directly into `~/Library/Application Support/com.apple.TCC/TCC.db` via `sqlite3 execSync`, granting `kTCCServiceSystemPolicySysAdminFiles` and `kTCCServiceSystemPolicyAppData` to Terminal, Node, and /usr/bin/python3 to bypass user-consent privacy prompts. The downloaded Python payload targets crypto-wallet libraries to steal seed phrases.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
